claim_boundary: Alignment proves only current row-level parity among declared obligations, owner code, and supplied evidence; it does not prove unmodeled behavior or production conformance.
do_not_use_when: The task is test/code/model partitioning, conformance replay, or the obligations have not yet been defined.

downstream_targets.condition: Required transition, leaf, payload, or replay evidence is large, slow, layered, stale-prone, background, or release-only.
Alignment exposes a missing/coarse/stale model obligation or boundary.
Alignment rows support a broad final claim.
Cross-guard or broad closure consumes the alignment result.

native_checks.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report
native_checks.summary: Run the model-code-test alignment model and known-bad bindings.

route_summary: Bind explicit model obligations to owner external code contracts and current same-behavior test, source, boundary, transition, payload, or replay evidence.

source_requirements.summary: Every required behavior binds one model obligation, owner external code contract, and current same-contract test evidence.
Concrete source/payload/counterexample claims require current target-aware audit, boundary, or external execution evidence.
Large or slow evidence is handed to TestMesh with target ids; MTA retains semantic binding ownership and does not count delegated work as pass.

use_when: Obligations, owner code contracts, source audits, boundary observations, payload cases, closure targets, or ordinary tests need direct current comparison.

workflow.summary: Define claim scope, commitments, obligations, required test kinds, and external behavior boundaries.
Inventory owner CodeContracts, tests, source audits, transitions/fields/payloads, boundary observations, and evidence targets.
Consume canonical cases and build row-level model-code-test bindings with stable target ids and gaps.
Run the alignment model; send large/slow evidence to TestMesh through typed handoff while retaining semantic ownership.
Return binding summaries and block broad coverage on stale, missing, orphan, duplicate, internal-only, or overclaimed rows.
